cross_scene_arcs.title: The Burden of Innocence and the Weight of Truth
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: This arc traces the central moral and existential conflict surrounding John Coffey's wrongful conviction and execution. From his arrest at the crime scene to the final revelation of his innocence, Coffey remains a passive vessel of truth in a system blind to justice. Despite overwhelming circumstantial evidence, his emotional distress and supernatural acts of healing point to his purity, culminating in Paul's painful realization that he is complicit in executing an innocent man. The arc underscores the tragedy of institutional failure and the cost of moral silence.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Coffey is arrested as the prime suspect in the murder of the Detterick twins based on circumstantial evidence and appearance.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Coffey is executed despite his innocence being known to Paul and the guards, leaving them haunted by their complicity.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The truth of Coffey's innocence cannot be publicly acknowledged due to lack of admissible evidence and systemic constraints, leaving the injustice unrectified.

cross_scene_arcs.title: The Divine Healer: Power, Purpose, and Sacrifice
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: This arc follows Coffey's journey as a supernatural healer whose power is intrinsically tied to suffering and self-sacrifice. Beginning with his first act of healing Paul's infection, Coffey's abilities are recognized and eventually leveraged to save Melinda Moores. Each use of his gift exacts a physical and spiritual toll, reinforcing his Christ-like role as a savior who bears the pain of others. His ultimate restoration of strength through the transfer of evil to Percy completes his transformation from passive inmate to divine agent of justice and healing.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Coffey heals Paul's urinary infection, revealing his supernatural ability and the personal cost it extracts.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Coffey regains strength by transferring Wild Bill's evil into Percy, completing his final act of divine intervention before his execution.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The source and full nature of Coffey's powers remain unexplained, as does the long-term consequence of Percy's mental destruction and the moral implications of Coffey's final act.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Coffey and Paul: A Bond Forged in Grace and Grief
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: This arc charts the deepening emotional and spiritual bond between John Coffey and Paul Edgecomb. Beginning with Paul's nostalgic recollection, the relationship evolves through acts of healing, shared vulnerability, and moral reckoning. Coffey's kindness and Paul's growing awareness of his innocence create a profound connection that transcends the prison walls. Even after Coffey's death, his legacy lives on through Paul's extended life, symbolizing the enduring impact of grace and compassion in a broken world.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Paul begins recounting his past, introducing Coffey as a transformative figure in his life, marked by emotional weight and unresolved grief.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Paul lives on for decades due to Coffey's indirect gift, carrying the memory and moral burden of their bond into old age.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Paul struggles with the emotional burden of immortality and outliving loved ones, haunted by guilt and the unresolved moral cost of Coffey's execution.

cross_scene_arcs.title: From Condemned to Redeemer: The Spiritual Journey
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: This arc captures Coffey's spiritual transcendence as he moves from the dehumanizing role of death row inmate to a figure of divine grace. His journey outside the prison—through the tunnel, into the woods, and to the Moore house—mirrors a sacred pilgrimage. His childlike wonder at fireflies, his reverence for beauty in film, and his final vision of the healed Detterick girls affirm his purity and otherworldly nature. Despite being condemned by man, Coffey achieves spiritual victory through love, healing, and peaceful acceptance of death.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Coffey enters the execution chamber, overwhelmed by the accumulated pain of past executions, signaling his deep spiritual sensitivity.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Coffey dies serenely, having shared a vision of redemption and healing, affirming his innocence and divine nature.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The world remains unchanged by Coffey's sacrifice, and the systemic capacity for hatred and injustice persists despite his evident goodness.

cross_scene_arcs.title: The Burden of Truth: Paul's Moral Reckoning with the Death Penalty
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Paul's growing disillusionment with the death penalty intensifies through a series of traumatic executions and moral crises, culminating in his recognition of Coffey's innocence and his own complicity in a profound injustice.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Paul witnesses Bitterbuck's painful execution, beginning to question the humanity of capital punishment.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Paul carries out Coffey's execution despite knowing he is innocent, shattered by the moral weight of his role.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Paul remains trapped by institutional constraints and lack of evidence, unable to prevent the execution of an innocent man, leaving his moral conflict unresolved and internalized.

cross_scene_arcs.title: From Silence to Revelation: Paul's Journey of Confession and Immortality
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Paul transitions from decades of silence to finally revealing the truth of his past, his supernatural longevity, and the miracle of John Coffey, forging a deep bond of trust with Elaine as the sole witness to his burden.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: After sixty years, Paul begins to open up to Elaine about his past on the Green Mile.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Paul fully discloses his immortality and the truth behind Mr. Jingles, entrusting Elaine with the legacy of Coffey's miracle.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Brad Dolan's hostility and the new lock on the shack threaten Paul's ability to maintain his connection to Mr. Jingles and the last living symbol of Coffey's grace.

cross_scene_arcs.title: The Quest for Redemption: Paul's Shift from Enforcer to Moral Guardian
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Paul evolves from a dutiful prison officer into a moral agent who defies the system to protect the innocent, culminating in his attempt to save Coffey and ultimately accept his fate with dignity.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Paul is attacked by Billy Wharton and healed by Coffey, awakening his awareness of supernatural justice and moral responsibility.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Paul accepts Coffey's choice to return and face death, recognizing it as an act of mercy rather than punishment.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Despite his efforts, Paul cannot prevent Coffey's execution, leaving his redemption incomplete and his moral mission unfulfilled.

cross_scene_arcs.title: The Weight of Suffering: Paul's Physical and Emotional Collapse
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Paul's deteriorating health and emotional repression culminate in a breakdown that forces him to confront both his physical condition and the psychological toll of his past, leading to a complete emotional release and transformation.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Paul collapses in pain, hiding the severity of his medical crisis from his family.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Paul breaks down sobbing in Jan's arms, finally releasing years of suppressed trauma and abandoning his role in executions.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The long-term consequences of Paul's decision to leave his post and how he will rebuild his life remain uncertain.

cross_scene_arcs.title: The Innocent and the Guilty: Uncovering the Truth Behind the Detterick Murders
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Paul's investigation into Coffey's past and the true nature of the Detterick murders leads him to uncover the truth—Wild Bill Wharton's guilt—yet he remains powerless to act, trapped between knowledge and institutional impotence.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Paul begins questioning Burt about Coffey's history, seeking evidence of innocence.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Paul confirms Wild Bill was at the crime scene and understands Coffey is innocent, but cannot stop the execution.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The truth is known but cannot be proven or acted upon, leaving Paul morally paralyzed and complicit in a state-sanctioned murder.

cross_scene_arcs.title: The Cruelty of Control: Percy's War on Innocence
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Percy's escalating campaign against the mouse symbolizes his deeper conflict with innocence and resistance, revealing his psychological fragility and need to dominate the powerless. Each confrontation exposes his incompetence and moral decay, culminating in the murder of Mr. Jingles, which triggers Coffey's supernatural intervention and marks the turning point in Percy's exposure as irredeemably cruel.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Percy reacts violently to the mouse, revealing insecurity and a need to assert dominance.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Percy kills Mr. Jingles, exposing his malice and triggering Coffey's miracle, which morally isolates him from the other guards.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Percy remains on the block despite his demonstrated cruelty, foreshadowing further abuse and the guards' inability to stop him.

cross_scene_arcs.title: From Obsession to Participation: Percy's Descent into Execution Rituals
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Percy's growing fascination with executions evolves from passive observation to active, malicious participation. His desire for power and recognition drives him to demand a central role, culminating in his sabotage of Del's execution. This arc traces his transformation from peripheral observer to active perpetrator of institutional violence.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Percy observes the execution rehearsal with curiosity, seeking to understand the mechanics of death.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Percy deliberately withholds the brine-soaked sponge, causing Del's agonizing death and revealing his sadistic intent.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Percy faces no immediate consequences, leaving his potential for future harm unchecked.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Isolation and Humiliation: The Collapse of Percy's Authority
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Percy's status erodes progressively as his actions alienate him from both guards and inmates. From being banished from the block to being mocked, overpowered, and ultimately restrained, this arc charts his fall from a position of petty authority to complete powerlessness and public disgrace.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Percy is banished from the Green Mile by Paul after asserting authority through cruelty.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Percy is gagged, bound, and locked in the restraint room, stripped of all authority and voice.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Whether Percy will retaliate upon release, leveraging his claimed connections, remains a looming threat.

cross_scene_arcs.title: The Psychological Unraveling: From Cowardice to Catatonia
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Percy's psychological decline begins with physical vulnerability and cowardice under pressure, accelerates through repeated humiliations, and culminates in his mental collapse after Coffey transfers Wild Bill's evil into him. This arc blends personal failure, moral corruption, and supernatural retribution, resulting in his complete psychological disintegration.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Percy suffers a painful urinary blockage, marking the onset of physical and emotional vulnerability.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Percy is permanently institutionalized in a catatonic state, symbolizing the irreversible consequences of his moral and psychological downfall.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The extent of Percy's self-awareness and whether he faces any internal reckoning remains unexplored.
